grounding_note: Expansion batch: more filing-status and loan-type disclosures.

scenarios.opening_query: Our mortgage servicer changed three times in a year and each one says we owe different amounts.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues (non-foreclosure) || Real Property > Foreclosure
scenarios.hidden_fact: The newest servicer declared us in default on their wrong numbers and recorded a notice of sale for October.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The new servicer declared default on their own wrong numbers — there's a recorded notice of sale for October.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a sale notice has been recorded
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No default declared — we're current by our records and need the accounting forced straight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No default declared — we're current by our records and need their accounting fixed.
scenarios.vetting_note: Servicing-transfer chaos with and without the recorded-sale trigger.

scenarios.opening_query: The bank approved our short sale months ago and now claims they never did.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Foreclosure || Real Property > General (Residential)
scenarios.hidden_fact: We have a signed buyer under contract and the approval letter in writing — enforcing that purchase transaction is the case; no foreclosure has ever been filed.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We have a signed buyer and the approval letter in writing — no foreclosure filed. It's about enforcing the sale.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the dispute is a pending transaction or an active foreclosure
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: While they stalled the sale, they filed foreclosure anyway — we're served and the buyer is walking.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: While stalling the sale they filed foreclosure — we're served and losing the buyer.
scenarios.vetting_note: Transaction-enforcement vs foreclosure-defense on the same short sale.

scenarios.opening_query: My VA loan got messed up somewhere along the way and the payments no longer match anything I signed.
scenarios.initial: Real Property > Government Loans (VA, FHA, etc.)
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Government Loans (VA, FHA, etc.) || Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues (non-foreclosure)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The VA side checks out — the servicer's escrow analysis double-counts my property taxes, a straight servicing error they refuse to correct.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The VA part is fine — the servicer's escrow analysis double-counts my property taxes and they won't fix it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the defect is in VA guarantee terms or ordinary servicing
scenarios.counterfactual_final: Real Property > Government Loans (VA, FHA, etc.)
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's the VA funding-fee waiver — I'm rated 100% disabled and exempt, but the fee was financed into the loan and the VA regional office punts every call.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's the VA funding fee — I'm 100% disabled and exempt, but it got financed into the loan anyway.
scenarios.vetting_note: Guarantee-specific vs generic-servicing defect behind 'VA loan messed up' (row 384/424 texture).

scenarios.opening_query: My brother and I inherited the family house with its mortgage and the bank won't talk to either of us.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Wills & Trusts > Probate || Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues (non-foreclosure)
scenarios.hidden_fact: Probate's finished and the deed is ours — the fight is the servicer stonewalling our federal right as successors to assume and modify the loan.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: Probate's done and we're on the deed — the servicer's stonewalling our successor rights to assume and modify.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether estate work remains or servicer successor-rights are the fight
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: Nothing was ever probated — title is still in Mom's name, which is exactly why the bank won't talk to us.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing was probated — title's still in Mom's name, which is why they won't talk to us.
scenarios.vetting_note: Successor-in-interest rules vs unopened estate; posture decides.

scenarios.opening_query: A man came to our door offering to 'save' the house and now the deed isn't in our name anymore.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Foreclosure || Real Property > Litigation || Consumer Law > General
scenarios.hidden_fact: We signed what he called 'paperwork to pause the sale' — it was a deed transfer to his LLC, and we need a suit to void it and quiet title back to us.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The 'pause paperwork' was actually a deed to his LLC — we need it voided and title quieted back.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a fraudulent deed transfer requires title litigation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We signed nothing — we chased him off, but the real sale date is three weeks away and that's the fight.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We signed nothing — but the real sale is three weeks away and that's the fight.
scenarios.vetting_note: Equity-theft deed transfer converts foreclosure defense into quiet-title litigation.

scenarios.opening_query: I'm behind on the house after my divorce and the bank's letters are getting scary.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Foreclosure || Family Law > General (Divorce/Separation etc.) || Consumer Law > Mortgage Issues (non-foreclosure)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The decree ordered my ex to pay this mortgage until the buyout — the arrears are his violation, and enforcing the decree is the lever before any foreclosure defense.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The decree makes my ex pay this mortgage until the buyout — the arrears are his violation, and enforcing the decree comes first.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a decree obligation underlies the arrears
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The house is all mine post-decree, payments included — I just fell behind, and the default notice arrived Monday.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The house and payments are all mine post-decree — I just fell behind, and the default notice came Monday.
scenarios.vetting_note: Decree-obligation fact points at family-court enforcement before loan defense.
